run 8f3a   step 7   KILLED (chaos harness) run 8f3a   resume   step 7 → 10   OK · no duplicate side effects

Three failures → three fixes

  1. Amnesia → Save points.

    Every step saves progress; a crash resumes, never restarts.

  2. Duplication → Exactly once.

    Recorded effects run once; an uncertain send halts loudly instead of re-firing — even after power loss.

  3. Hallucination → Citations.

    Claims about your codebase cite real files, or are flagged as guesses.

How it works

  1. 01

    Describe it

    Plain English. No graph vocabulary required.

  2. 02

    Approve the blueprint

    One screen: workers, handoffs, save points, stop rules.

  3. 03

    It builds the project

    A runnable, zero-dependency project. Runs with no API keys.

  4. 04

    It PROVES it

    A chaos test kills it mid-run and verifies recovery.

Proof, not promises

Kill-tested

A chaos harness crashes it on purpose and asserts recovery.

Red-teamed by rival AIs

GPT-5.1, Gemini 2.5 Pro, DeepSeek R1 held the release twice until every finding was fixed or disclosed. Reports in the repo.

No lock-in

MIT licensed · zero dependencies · runs with no API keys.
